articles.headline: Stock Market News for Jun 18, 2026
articles.summary: U.S. equities closed sharply lower on June 17 after the Federal Reserve held rates steady at 3.50-3.75% under new Chair Kevin Warsh but signaled potential rate hikes. Dow fell 507.12 pts (-1.0%) to 51,492.55; Nasdaq dropped 354.69 pts (-1.3%) to 26,021.66; S&P 500 declined 1.2% to 7,420.10. All 11 S&P 500 sectors fell. CVNA plunged 10.3%. VIX rose 12.4% to 18.44.

articles.headline: Fed Holds Steady in Warsh's Debut, but Hawkish Shift Fuels Bond-Market Rout
articles.summary: FOMC held rates at 3.50-3.75% on a 12-0 vote under new Chair Kevin Warsh. Nine officials project at least one rate hike by year-end 2026 (median shifted from cuts to one hike). Core PCE 2026 raised to 3.3% from 2.7%. Policy statement removed easing bias. Warsh emphasized no forward guidance and announced five task forces on communications, balance sheet, data sources, productivity, and inflation measurement. 10Y yield spiked 7bps to 4.495%; 2Y +17bps to 4.216%. S&P 500 fell 1.3%. Fed funds futures priced 72% chance of October hike.

articles.headline: US 10 Year Treasury Note Yield — Eased to 4.45% on June 18
articles.summary: US 10Y yield at 4.451%, down 5bp from prior session after rising nearly 5bp post-FOMC. 2Y at 4.18%. Curve: 10Y-2Y ~27bp. Fed held rates at 3.75% but half of FOMC expects at least one hike. Trump signed interim Iran peace deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz. Oil dropped sharply on the announcement. CPI (May) at 4.20%. Fed funds rate at 3.75%. Unemployment 4.30%.

articles.headline: Accenture, Kroger And 3 Stocks To Watch Heading Into Thursday
articles.summary: U.S. stock futures higher Thursday morning. ACN (BMO): EPS est .71, rev 8.76B, AH +1.4% to 58.21. KR (BMO): EPS est .59, rev 5.47B, AH +0.1% to 1.82. SWBI (reported Wed close): EPS /usr/bin/bash.36 vs /usr/bin/bash.23 est, rev 78.4M vs 55.3M, AH +18.6%. SB (Safe Bulkers): beat, AH +3.4%. SNBR: Nasdaq delisting after Ch. 11 bankruptcy, AH -31%.

articles.headline: After Hours Stock Movers — Jun 17, 2026
articles.summary: After-hours index moves: S&P 500 +0.63%, Nasdaq 100 +1.03%, Dow +0.41%, Russell 2000 +0.88%. Top gainers: SWBI +18.6% (earnings beat), RUM +17.0%, ACN +1.4%. Top losers: SNBR -31.0% (Ch. 11 delisting). Jabil (JBL) EPS .16 beat .08 est, rev .8B beat .55B.

articles.headline: E-Mini S&P 500 Jun 26 (ES=F) — Pre-Market Data
articles.summary: ES futures: Last 7,492.75, Open 7,512.50, Range 7,504.25-7,568.25, Volume 130.78k. 5D return +2.13%, 1M +1.72%, 6M +12.29%, YTD +8.77%, 1Y +26.28%. Key news: futures climbing with focus on Iran deal and Fed hike path.

articles.headline: Pre-market Stock Trading — CNN: S&P 500 Futures +1.33%
articles.summary: S&P 500 Futures 7,533.75 +98.75 (+1.33%) as of 8:14 AM ET on June 17 data feed. Futures pointing to a strong opening bounce after Wednesday's FOMC-driven sell-off.

articles.headline: Oil settles at three-month low after Trump says deal signed to end Iran war
articles.summary: WTI crude fell 5.8% to 9.93/bbl after Trump signed interim peace deal with Iran to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Oil down 20% from 2026 peak. Iran lowered OSP for light crude to Asian buyers to .15 above Oman/Dubai average. Pakistan mediated the MoU. Strait of Hormuz reopens under the deal.

articles.headline: 9 Fed Officials Signal 2026 Rate Hike; Warsh Drops Forward Guidance
articles.summary: Fed voted 12-0 to hold at 3.50-3.75%. 9 of 18 dot plot participants projected a hike by year-end — complete hawkish reversal from March dot plot (which projected cuts). PCE inflation 2026 raised to 3.6%. Bitcoin fell on the hawkish signal. Crypto stocks MSTR, COIN, HOOD moved on the Fed reaction.

articles.headline: DXY at Crucial Level Ahead of Fed; Tests 99.50 Support
articles.summary: DXY testing major support near 99.50 ahead of Fed decision. Post-Fed spike took DXY to 100.47 (+0.9%). DXY at 99.65 on June 17 close. Monthly +0.46%, yearly +0.76%.

articles.headline: Jobless Claims Preview: Next Release June 18, 8:30 AM ET
articles.summary: Initial jobless claims last week (Jun 6): 229K (up from 225K prior). Continuing claims: 1,795K (up from 1,771K). Next release: Jun 18 at 8:30 AM ET. Previous unadjusted claims: 228,276. Seasonal factors expected increase of 35,595.

articles.headline: CVNA Falls 10.3% as Broad Market Sells Off; Zacks Maintains Strong Buy
articles.summary: CVNA closed at 0.04, up 1.65% on June 17 from prior day, but plunged 10.3% in the overall June 17 session from previous levels. Daily range: 7.20-3.55. Zacks maintains Rank #1 (Strong Buy). Expanding into new vehicle sales.

articles.headline: MSTR Buys 00M Bitcoin; Holdings Now 846.84K BTC
articles.summary: Strategy (MSTR) acquired 1,587 BTC for 00M via ATM share sale. Total holdings now 846.84K BTC. BTC trading near 6K. MSTR stock up 3.18% on June 15. Crypto stocks broadly higher: COIN -0.41%, HOOD +1.04%, MARA +3.45%.

articles.headline: SMCI Stock Jumps 9% Despite Oracle Canceling .4B Contract
articles.summary: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) stock jumped 9% despite Oracle reportedly canceling a contract estimated between .1B and .4B. Market interpreted the cancellation as non-material or already priced in.
